task_completion.description: Verify the recipient is set correctly in the email composition window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open email composition window with 'staff@corp.example.com' entered in the 'To' or 'Recipient' field.

task_completion.description: Verify the subject line is set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Q3 Townhall Update' entered in the 'Subject' field of the email composition window.

task_completion.description: Verify the company logo image is inserted into the email body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an actual image (header_logo.png) rendered inside the rich text body of the email composition window.

task_completion.description: Verify a 3x2 table is inserted into the email body after the logo.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a rendered table grid (3 columns by 2 rows, or 3 rows by 2 columns) located below the inserted logo in the email body.

task_completion.description: Verify the email was saved as a draft and the composition window was closed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the disappearance of the email composition window in the final screenshots, and ideally the main email client window showing the 'Drafts' folder containing the 'Q3 Townhall Update' email.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a graphical email client to compose the message.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the screenshots show a standard email client GUI (like Thunderbird, Evolution, or webmail) rather than a plain text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used rich text/HTML formatting rather than typing raw HTML code.
integrity.visual_evidence: The email body should show a visually rendered image and a graphical table grid, NOT raw HTML tags like or typed out as plain text.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that there are no terminal windows open showing commands that directly inject drafts into the mail directory or use command-line mail utilities.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected recipient is 'staff@corp.example.com' and the expected subject is 'Q3 Townhall Update'.
